description_long: This object is a vintage wall-mounted corded telephone, featuring a rectangular backplate with a prominent black handset attached by a long, coiled cord. The main body of the phone is compact and boxy, with two circular earpiece and mouthpiece components on the handset. The handset rests on a cradle that protrudes from the main unit, and the cord dangles freely, suggesting it is designed for frequent use. The front of the phone appears to have a rotary dial or button interface, though the details are partially obscured by the handset. The overall construction is robust, with a combination of metal and plastic materials, and the design is utilitarian, typical of public or industrial telephones.
description: A vintage wall-mounted corded telephone with a black handset, coiled cord, and sturdy rectangular backplate. The handset rests on a cradle, and the phone features a classic, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the handset on its cradle, the coiled cord, and the main body of the phone mounted on a rectangular backplate.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the slim profile of the backplate and the protruding handset and cradle assembly.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying only the plain, rectangular backplate with no visible phone components.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handset and cord hanging from the cradle, with the phone's body extending slightly from the backplate.
